Overview of the events of 2024 in heavy metal music

This is a timeline documenting the events of heavy metal in the year 2024.

Bands disbanded[edit]

  • Destrage[1]
  • Issues[2]
  • Sum 41[3]

Events[edit]

  • On January 13, 3 Inches of Blood will perform their first show in nine years at the Commodore Ballroom in Vancouver.[4]
  • On April 6, 2023, Nightwish announced that they will go on a hiatus following the release of their upcoming tenth studio album in 2024.[5]
  • On July 20, the Klash of the Ruhrpott festival is scheduled to take place at Amphitheater Gelsenkirchen in Gelsenkirchen, Germany, which would unite Kreator, Sodom, Destruction and Tankard, known as "the Big Teutonic Four", for the first time on one stage.[6]
  • Cirith Ungol will retire from performing live at the end of this year.[7]
  • Chimaira will perform at the 2024 Inkcarceration Fest, their first festival concert in 14 years.[8][9]
  • In January, Issues will split up following three farewell concerts after a three-year hiatus without vocalist Tyler Carter, who was fired from the band for multiple accusations of grooming and sexual misconduct.[2]
  • In March, Sepultura will begin what is announced as a year-and-a-half long farewell tour in celebration of their 40th anniversary.[10]

Albums expected[edit]

January[edit]

Day Artist Album
12 Emil Bulls Love Will Fix It[11]
Exit Eden Femmes Fatales[12]
Project 86 OMNI, Pt. 2[13]
The Rods Rattle the Cage[14]

Scanner The Cosmic Race[16]
19 Master Saints Dispelled[17]
Saxon Hell, Fire and Damnation[18]
26 Any Given Day Limitless[19]
Caligula's Horse Charcoal Grace[20]
Inquisition Veneration of Medieval Mysticism and Cosmological Violence[21]
Kill the Thrill Autophagie[22]
Lucifer Lucifer V[23]
Madder Mortem Old Eyes, New Heart[24]
Mägo de Oz Alicia en el Metalverso[25]
Manticora Mycelium[26]
Static-X Project: Regeneration Vol. 2[27]

February[edit]

Day Artist Album
2 Artillery Raw Live (At Copenhell) (live album)[29]
Enterprise Earth Death: An Anthology[30]
Ghoul Noxious Concoctions (EP)[31]

Necrowretch Swords of Dajjal[33]

Striker Ultrapower[35]
9 Chelsea Wolfe She Reaches Out to She Reaches Out to She[36]
Infected Rain Time[37]
The Last Ten Seconds of Life No Name Graves[38]
Les Claypool Adverse Yaw: The Prawn Song Years (box set)[39]
Morbid Saint Swallowed by Hell[40]
Per Wiberg The Serpent's Here[41]
16 Ihsahn Ihsahn[42]
The Obsessed Gilded Sorrow[43]
Riot V Mean Streets[44]
See You Next Tuesday Relapses (remix album)[45]
23 Ace Frehley 10,000 Volts[46]
Amaranthe The Catalyst[47]
Austrian Death Machine Quad Brutal[48]
Borknagar Fall[49]
Darkest Hour Perpetual I Terminal[50]
Job for a Cowboy Moon Healer[51]
Lionheart The Grace of a Dragonfly[52]
Mick Mars The Other Side of Mars[53]

Day Artist Album
1 Blind Channel Exit Emotions[54]

Firewind Stand United[56]

New Years Day Half Black Heart[59]

8 Judas Priest Invincible Shield[61]
Sonata Arctica Clear Cold Beyond[62]
15 DragonForce Warp Speed Warriors[63]
Necrophobic In the Twilight Grey[64]
Scott Stapp Higher Power[65]
22 Alestorm Voyage of the Dead Marauder (EP)[66]
29 Rage Afterlifelines[67]
Sum 41 Heaven :x: Hell[68]
While She Sleeps Self Hell[69]

April[edit]

Day Artist Album
5 Korpiklaani Rankarumpu[70]

  2. ^ a b Greg Kennely (November 17, 2023). "ISSUES Announces Breakup & Final Three Shows". Metal Injection. Retrieved November 20, 2023.
  3. ^ Greg Kennely (November 30, 2023). "DERYCK WHIBLEY Explains Why SUM 41 Is Breaking Up". Metal Injection. Retrieved November 30, 2023.
